about.title: About TRK Agriculture
about.subtitle: Plants grown in Mauritius, by a passionate family
about.intro: TRK Agriculture is a Mauritian family nursery. We grow a wide range of vegetable plants right here in Henrietta (Vacoas): herbs and spices, salads, brèdes, assorted vegetables, melons and watermelons.
about.body: Our belief is simple: a good garden starts with a good plant. That's why we grow healthy, ready-to-transplant plants locally — no imports, no long supply chains. Straight from the grower to you.

approach.title: Our approach
approach.body: Every tray is carefully prepared in our nursery. We select varieties suited to the Mauritian climate to give you the best chance of success, whether you're a hobby gardener or an experienced grower.

delivery.title: Delivery across the island
delivery.body: We deliver your plants to all 9 districts of Mauritius, straight to your door. Choose your plants, place your order, and receive them fresh and ready to plant.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.intro: A question about our plants, an order, or a project? We're here to help.
contact.note: For orders, the easiest way is through our online catalogue. For bulk orders (hotels, restaurants, resellers), contact us directly via WhatsApp or email.

process.title: Our Process
process.subtitle: From substrate to your garden — how our seedlings come to life
process.plateauDesc: All our seedlings are grown in cell trays in a controlled environment. Each cell holds a single seed, carefully nurtured until it reaches the ideal stage for transplanting into your garden.
process.standard128Desc: Standard format for herbs, salads, brèdes and vegetables
process.cucu98Desc: Larger cells for cucurbits: melons, watermelons, cucumbers

substrat.title: Substrate preparation
substrat.desc: We select and blend peat, perlite and organic compost tailored to each plant family. The substrate ensures ideal drainage and water retention.

semis.title: Seeding
semis.desc: One seed is placed per cell at the optimal depth for each species. Every seed is selected for its high germination rate.

germination.title: Germination
germination.desc: Under controlled conditions (temperature, humidity), seeds sprout in 3 to 14 days depending on the variety and season.

croissance.title: Nursery growth
croissance.desc: Seedlings grow under shelter with regular watering and pest monitoring. This is the longest stage of the production cycle.

durcissement.title: Hardening off
durcissement.desc: Before sale, plants are progressively exposed to outdoor conditions — sunlight, wind, temperature changes — to strengthen their resilience.

vente.title: Ready to sell
vente.desc: The plant is vigorous, well-rooted in its cell, and ready to be transplanted into your garden bed, pot or raised planter.

process.durationsNote: Approximate timelines in Mauritius. The hot season (Nov – Apr) speeds up growth; the cool season (May – Oct) slows it slightly.
ideasContent: Planting tips articles — week-by-week guides on caring for your seedlings after delivery
FAQ — your most common questions: watering, substrate, pests common in Mauritius
Our Story page — a portrait of the TRK family and our nursery in Henrietta (Vacoas)
Nursery videos — follow production from seed to seedling in time-lapse
process.ideasVarietiesTitle: New varieties to grow by season
ideasVarieties: Premium herbs: peppermint, spearmint, oregano, rosemary (cool season, May – Oct)
Edible flowers: nasturtiums, borage, marigolds (year-round, ideal for restaurants)
Extra hot-season brèdes: mafane leaves (antiparasitic), tropical watercress (Nov – Apr)
Creole chillies: bird's eye chilli, banana pepper, sweet chilli (hot season, Nov – Apr)
Microgreens & sprouts: radish, sunflower, chickpea (ultra-fast crop, 7–14 days)

calendrier.title: Seasonal Calendar
calendrier.subtitle: The best months to order each category in Mauritius
calendrier.climateNote: Mauritius · Hot & rainy season: Nov → Apr · Cool & dry season: May → Oct
calendrier.note: This calendar reflects seasonal nursery production. Off-season orders are possible on request.
